latin: Post theam in mare proiectam, rex Britannicus portum Bostoniensem clausit. 'punientur,' dixit. sed coloni non territi sunt — potius uniti sunt. quod rex frangere volebat, fortius fecit.

questions.prompt: How did the British king respond to the tea party?
What was the unintended effect of the punishment?

translation: After the tea was thrown into the sea, the British king closed the port of Boston. 'They will be punished,' he said. But the colonists were not frightened — rather they were united. What the king wanted to break, he made stronger.
